title: hieracium lansicum subsp. reboudianum
intro: Hieracium lansicum subsp. reboudianum, also known as Rebour's hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium lansicum plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium lansicum subsp. reboudianum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is also used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
